title: Function Arguments
description: How to pass data into your functions.

slides.title: Feeding the Machine 🍔
slides.content:
## Inputs (Arguments)

A function that always does the *exact* same thing isn't very useful.

We usually want to give a function some data to work with. We do this by defining **parameters** inside the parentheses `()`.

quizData.question: What is the purpose of function parameters?

options.text: To act as the return result of the code
To allow external data/inputs into the function's code block

quizData.explanation: Parameters define what data a function expects to receive when it is called.

slides.title: Type After Name 🏷️
slides.content:
Just like variables, when you define a parameter, you must state its type *after* its name.

`func greet(name string)` says: *"I expect to receive text, and I will call it 'name' inside my block."*

quizData.question: Which of these correctly defines a function that takes a number called 'score'?
quizData.explanation: Go enforces strong typing. The identifier (`score`) must be followed by its exact type (`int`).
